problem: src/output/pdf/detail_modules/indicators.rs::render_tech_stack called finding.severity.label() unconditionally in the findings table's severity column, always emitting the German label ("Hoch"/"Kritisch") even when the report's locale was English.
evidence: Found during the same locale-leak sweep as seo-details-locale-leak; fixed by introducing a locale-aware `let en = i18n.locale() == "en";` binding and branching to finding.severity.label_en() ("HIGH"/"CRITICAL") for English reports.
expected: Every severity label rendered into the PDF selects label() vs label_en() based on the active report locale, never label() unconditionally.
counter_examples: A German-locale report with the same StackFinding must still show "Kritisch", not "CRITICAL" — this is a locale-selection fix, not a hardcoding of English.
